reasoning: This sequence represents the classic 'shrug' gesture, expressing confusion, indifference, or uncertainty. The arms move in sync, raising slightly to mimic the raising of shoulders and turning the palms outward to emphasize the empty handed helplessness or questioning nature of the pose. I’ll reference other examples to ensure the shrug feels natural and fluid, with slight variations in the wrist positions to maintain realism. In the first frame, both arms are relaxed at the sides, with Joint1 rolling the arms slightly inward and Joint2 pitching them downward to simulate a neutral position. The wrists are neutral as well, palms facing inward. In the second frame, the arms start to lift outward as the robot begins the shrugging motion. Joint1 rotates outward and upward, and Joint2 pitches the arms forward slightly, while the wrists rotate to -90°, turning the palms upward. This creates the 'what can I do?' look. By the third frame, the arms are slightly higher, as if completing the shrug. Joint1 continues to lift the arms outward, with Joint2 adjusting the pitch slightly to increase the height of the shrug. The wrists remain in the upward-facing position, adding emphasis to the gesture. In the fourth frame, the arms are fully raised, completing the shrug. Joint1 and Joint2 work together to bring the arms upward to their peak position, with the shoulders lifting and the palms staying upward, perfectly capturing the essence of a shrug. Finally, in the fifth frame, the arms drop back down into a neutral position, with Joint1 and Joint2 resetting to their relaxed state. The wrists return to neutral, palms facing inward once again, indicating the end of the gesture.
